title: Torta Caprese
subtitle: A flourless Italian chocolate and almond cake from Capri
excerpt: Torta Caprese is a naturally flourless chocolate cake from the island of Capri. It is made entirely with dark chocolate, ground almonds, butter, eggs and sugar. The result is dense, fudgy and crisp on the outside with no gluten at all. Icing sugar dusted over the top is the only decoration.
sourceNotes: Torta Caprese is a traditional cake from the island of Capri in Campania, southern Italy. The legend of its creation attributes it to a cook who accidentally forgot to add flour to a chocolate cake in the 1920s. The recipe is documented in Neapolitan and Campanian cooking texts and is considered a regional speciality of Capri. This recipe is synthesised from the established Italian tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Wrap the cooled cake well and freeze for up to 2 months. Thaw at room temperature. Dust with fresh icing sugar before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake keeps for 3 days at room temperature, well covered. It is fudgier and better on day two.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan or conventional; the cake bakes quickly and should be watched

glossaryTerms.definition: A flourless cake uses ground nuts or starch instead of wheat flour for structure. Without gluten, the cake cannot form the same network of strands that holds a conventional sponge together, so the texture is denser and fudgier. Torta Caprese is meant to be dense; it is not undercooked. The centre should feel set but give slightly when pressed.

content.text: with five ingredients. The almonds provide structure and a slight bitterness that works with the dark chocolate. There is nothing to hide imperfect technique: use the best dark chocolate you can find, at least 70% cocoa solids. The icing sugar dusted over the top before serving is traditional; anything more would compete with the simplicity of the cake.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C. Grease and line a 20 cm round tin. Melt the 200 g dark chocolate and 150 g butter together in a bowl set over barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 5 minutes. Stir in 100 g of the caster sugar, then stir in the 4 egg yolks one at a time. Fold in the 150 g ground almonds.
Fold in the whites and bake

In a clean bowl, whisk the 4 egg whites to soft peaks. Gradually whisk in the remaining 50 g caster sugar until the whites are stiff and glossy. Fold a third of the whites into the chocolate mixture to loosen it, then fold in the rest in two additions. Pour into the prepared tin and bake for 28 to 32 minutes until the edges are set and pulling away from the tin sides, the centre is just firm when pressed but still has a slight give, and a skewer comes out with a few moist crumbs. Cool in the tin for 30 minutes, then turn out carefully and dust with icing sugar before serving.

items.solution: A slight dip is normal for a flourless cake as it cools. A deep collapse usually means the centre was still liquid when removed from the oven. Ensure the edge is pulling away from the tin before taking it out. The internal temperature should reach 88 degrees C.

items.problem: Cake sticks and breaks when turning out
items.solution: The tin was not lined well enough. Line the base and sides with baking paper and run a thin knife around the edge before turning out. Allow a full 30 minutes to cool in the tin so the structure firms up.
